detect: settings.gradle(.kts) at repo root OR in android/, AND (iosApp/ dir OR a *.xcodeproj). Flutter (pubspec.yaml) and React Native (metro.config.js) are ruled out first — they carry both native trees and would false-positive.
gatesComment: Module paths assume the application module is :app — adjust per repo. Roborazzi task semantics: test tasks run tests and write NO baselines; recordRoborazziDebug regenerates them; verifyRoborazziDebug compares and FAILS on a diff. GOTCHA to check in any repo adopting this profile: a `roborazzi.test.record=true` line in gradle.properties makes EVERY test task silently re-record baselines as a side effect — the fast gate then rewrites goldens from current code before the snapshot gate compares them, so a genuine UI regression can never fail the gate. Remove that flag if present. Some baselines render nondeterministically across machines; list the known-noisy ones in the repo overlay's gatesComment and treat them as environmental noise unless a source change explains them.

gateNotes.snapshot: Baselines are VERIFIED, not rewritten. A changed EXISTING baseline is a real 'fail' — never re-record just to make it pass; re-record only for an intended change, saying plainly which surface changed and why. A NEW component with no baseline: run `./gradlew recordRoborazziDebug`, then report 'pass' with a detail NAMING every recorded file so a human can eyeball it. Popup-layer surfaces (DropdownMenu, ModalBottomSheet, AlertDialog, tooltips, coach marks) are UNREACHABLE by captureRoboImage — extract their content into a plain composable and snapshot THAT directly. Flag any new baseline that is blank, near-blank, or byte-identical to a sibling: identical siblings mean a fixture that does not vary, and blank means the composable rendered nothing.
gateNotes.e2e: First run `adb devices`; if a device/emulator is attached, run the e2e gate. If none is attached, report status 'skipped' with detail 'no emulator running'.

conventions: Kotlin Multiplatform (Android + iOS) app with Compose Multiplatform UI. Shared logic in commonMain; platform code in androidMain/iosMain. Follow the repo's existing module layout, DI, app-shell, and navigation patterns — read neighboring files before adding anything. Keep test ids / testTag strings in shared code so both platforms drive the same identifiers. Match surrounding naming, comment density, and idiom. No new dependencies without strong justification.

reviewDimensions.ask: Logic bugs, edge cases, null/empty/error handling, state that fails to reset between operations.
commonMain/platform split is correct, DI wiring matches the codebase, module boundaries respected, no dead code, no needless new dependencies.
Uses the project's design-system components, correct testTags for anything user-facing, snapshot-worthy states are covered by Roborazzi.

procedures.snapshot: Roborazzi tasks are the `Debug`-suffixed variants (verifyRoborazziDebug / recordRoborazziDebug). New composables need a baseline recorded first.
procedures.ios: This profile runs only the Android-side gates. Build the iOS framework and run the iOS UI tests separately (xcodebuild) before any release.
